notes: Bootstrap instance seeded by Evan Wave 5B-2 (2026-04-19) from the HY-IG v2 reference pair and the Tournament Design Parameters signal enumeration. Closes §1.2 of docs/validation-audit-20260419-evan.md (signal-code naming drift). Append-only: later waves extend this list as new pairs introduce new signals. Never reorder or delete existing entries.

signals.description: Posterior probability of the stress regime from a 2-state Gaussian Hidden Markov Model fit on the indicator series. Values in [0, 1]; higher = more stressed. Regime label 'stress' is assigned by pre-whitened variance ranking (larger-variance state = stress) per META-D2 sanity check.
signals.notes: Sign convention: stress = high. Direction is countercyclical for risk-off targets (SPY, equities). Validated against GFC / COVID episodes (stress_prob > 0.7 during both).

signals.description: Maximum a-posteriori regime label from a 3-state Gaussian Hidden Markov Model (expansion / transition / contraction). Integer-valued in {0, 1, 2}; labels assigned by variance ranking (lowest variance = expansion, highest = contraction).
signals.notes: Discrete state index, NOT a probability. Tournament strategies typically condition on mode==0 (expansion) vs mode==2 (contraction); transition state (mode==1) is often pooled with expansion for P1 / P2 strategy families.

signals.description: Smoothed probability of the high-variance (stress) regime from a 2-state Markov-switching regression with regime-dependent intercept and variance. Values in [0, 1]; higher = more stressed.
signals.notes: Distinct from hmm_stress: Markov-switching fits a regression (with covariates) rather than a marginal Gaussian mixture. Regime labeling follows the same variance-ranking convention as hmm_stress.
